Title: Indoloquinoline alkaloid neocryptolepine derivative inhibits Botrytis cinerea by targeting thiamine thiazole synthase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: THI4 family thiazole synthase; suicide enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Key branch enzyme in thiazole synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of the thiazole component of thiamine in the thiamine biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Thiazole component of thiamine; HET-P-related thiazole intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies thiazole precursor required for thiamine and TPP formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for glycolysis, TCA cycle, pentose phosphate pathway, branched-chain amino acid metabolism, and cellular energy metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly up-regulated under Z24 stress in B. cinerea Pers. and B05.10; alternative 5' splice site events up-regulated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibition of Bcthi4 by Z24 disrupts thiamine biosynthesis and suppresses B. cinerea growth; Bcthi4 expression rises as a compensatory response under Z24 stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DARTS target fishing; mass spectrometry; SPR binding assay; CETSA; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis of Met304/Thr232/Thr231; enzyme activity assay; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq/alternative splicing analysis; thiamine and TPP quantification; exogenous thiamine rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bcthi4 was identified as the primary direct target of antifungal compound Z24 by DARTS and mass spectrometry. 2. Z24 directly bound recombinant Bcthi4 with high affinity, and mutation of Met304/Thr232/Thr231 abolished binding. 3. Z24 inhibited Bcthi4 enzyme activity in both B. cinerea Pers. and B05.10 strains. 4. Z24 induced up-regulation of Bcthi4 transcription and alternative 5' splice-site events, indicating feedback compensation under thiamine stress. 5. Exogenous thiamine partially rescued fungal growth inhibition, supporting Bcthi4-mediated blockade of thiamine metabolism as the causal mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Z24 treatment; exogenous thiamine supplementation; in vitro PDA/liquid PDA culture; infection assays on tomato, cucumber, and grape
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/attractive antifungal target in fungal thiamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude Z24 is the first reported fungal Bcthi4 catalytic inhibitor. Transcriptomic evidence indicates Bcthi4 riboswitch-associated A5SS up-regulation under stress. Although thiamine and TPP levels increased under Z24 treatment, authors interpret this as compensatory feedback under limited thiamine synthesis rather than successful pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a fungicide target for crop protection rather than breeding; screening/design of Bcthi4 inhibitors may yield new anti-Botrytis fungicides with low cross-resistance risk
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential phytotoxicity at higher concentrations delayed radish bud growth, though no major seed germination effect was observed
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq for DEGs and alternative splicing; metabolomics by UPLC-Q Exactive MS; proteomics/mass spectrometry from DARTS bands
